text:
There was an elder woodpecker who lived high up in an old oak tree.

His name was Oliver.

Once upon an autumn morning, Oliver noticed some young birds settling in a freshly leafed field below.

"They seem hungry," he thought.

That afternoon, Oliver decided to share.

He tapped three acorns into small circles with his sharp beak.

Each small circle he placed was enough for just one little bird.

Descending slowly down into the field, Oliver fed them.

The young birds thanked him with excited chirps and grateful tweets.

That winter, Oliver survived a tough chill—a bite.

But thanks to the squirrels who saved him leftovers.

And the little birds who sang him a special song?

He fluttered his eyes closed, and fell right asleep.

And just like that, Oliver dreamed he was an orchard with beautiful apples.

When he woke up bright and early, he didn't know what he'd do.

Then he just thought about those little birds.

He'd find them some
